You can't pause the underperforming Campaigns and you can't scale up the performing campaigns. So this requires a lot of time and just manpower and But this is the most effective way to do the Facebook ads. So I'll give you an example You know when we do our when I used to do my targeting we take like you know a whole bunch of different popular bodybuilders And we just lump them into one campaign called popular bodybuilders The problem with that is when we start seeing sales From that campaign. We don't know if it's coming from the Arnold Schwarzenegger page if they're coming from the Dorian Yates page We don't know which one of those bodybuilders are Are which pages are actually producing those sales so when the campaign stops working We have to turn the whole thing off like we can't move pretty much. We can't just add more bodybuilders So what the way we would set up the campaign is It would be each each target would have it would be specific to its own country And that way we can find out like where the sales coming from. I mean I don't need the minds sharing this but like a little game-changing tip We've learned is that all of our upsell like the people are taking the 197 upsell off a seven dollar offer. They're all coming from Australia When we found that out we're like, you know, you know, we had all these different countries We knew New Zealand wasn't making any sales the UK wasn't making any sales. We turn that stuff We turn that off right away all of a sudden you start, you know You start getting closer to break even and you can leave that traffic on so you really have to go granular and That just requires more time setting up the campaign But you need to know is this coming off a desktop or is this coming off the mobile you can't lock those campaigns together The conversions are different And you want and again, it's really as simple as that in concept You know keeping a close eye on it and then turning off so we have a rule with the Facebook as long as we make back 50% of what we spend within 24 hours we leave the campaign on So if I spend three grand today and we made back 1500 we keep it going and even at the start I might even take a bigger loss But um, you know some people who might you know, we're starting up some of my wife's stuff and You know she we're not we're not We're not gonna be as flexible We're gonna have tighter criteria. So you kind of have to set yourself some criteria

## [39:30 - 42:34]

You may want to make back at least 80% or 75% but you know for most guys, you know, this is a new world for me, too I don't want to come across as a pro I'm learning this by you know burning a lot of money and you kind of have to be prepared to do that But we do start all of the campaigns off with only 10 bucks You know if we make our money if we make 50% of that back we keep it on and we go up in small increments over 25 bucks to 50 bucks So you know, we don't jump into big budgets and I don't think Facebook allows you to do that So one thing you can do if you want to do Facebook and you're making some sales It's you know if you got a clickbank or any shopping cart go in and look at where all your sales are coming from so export all the customer data into An Excel file and organize it by country you may find some like fascinating data You may find like a whole bunch of like you may find you just get a lot of sales from a specific city in a specific country And then you can target your your ads to those people so You know, it's just kind of reverse engineering using the data you have and just kind of being smart, but um You know that can save you a lot of money you create some momentum right off the back.
